name: rbfeeder-backdoor
summary: FORENSIC ANALYSIS: MALWARE ON RASPBERRY PI (RBFEEDER / RADARBOX)

documentation.readme: FORENSIC ANALYSIS: MALWARE ON RASPBERRY PI (RBFEEDER / RADARBOX)
usage_sections: User installs rbfeeder from the official RadarBox repository
A permanent connection to the AirNav server is established
A command is sent through the proprietary protocol
rbfeeder receives it and executes it via system shell (more on this later)
The command downloads and runs a malicious script from the domain apt.transponderlive.org

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'post_exploit' phase against surfaces [web, shell_windows, shell_linux] in the Command and Control category. Fits when the engagement needs: FORENSIC ANALYSIS: MALWARE ON RASPBERRY PI (RBFEEDER / RADARBOX).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Command and Control, use rbfeeder-backdoor because FORENSIC ANALYSIS: MALWARE ON RASPBERRY PI (RBFEEDER / RADARBOX).
When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use rbfeeder-backdoor as a cataloged Command and Control tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for rbfeeder-backdoor.
When chaining Command and Control tools, pair rbfeeder-backdoor with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `rbfeeder-backdoor --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
